title: 🔥 Change Failure Rate & Quality [v2]
description: DORA Pillar: Change Failure Rate — Measures the percentage of deployments that result in a degraded service. Elite teams keep CFR below 5%.

list.name: environment
list.label: Environment
list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment

panels.title: Data Source
panels.description: Shows the current data source mode (live, seed, or demo), the source repository or label, and a link to the source if available. Updates automatically after each sync.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '🟢 Live — ' || source_label
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🟠 Seed — ' || source_label
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo — ' || source_label
    ELSE '⚪ ' || mode || ' — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
  END AS "Data Source",
  COALESCE(source_url, '') AS "Source URL"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: Last Synced
panels.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful sync job. Updates automatically when a new sync completes.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT finished_at AS "Last Synced"
FROM sync_jobs
WHERE status = 'success'
ORDER BY finished_at DESC
LIMIT 1

panels.title: Change Fail Rate (%)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of successful deployments followed by an incident (labeled issue) within 24 hours in the selected environment.

### What insight it offers
Elite: <5%, High: <10%, Medium: <15%, Low: ≥15%. High CFR indicates insufficient testing or risky deployment practices.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Incidents identified by 'incident' label on GitHub Issues
- 24-hour window is an approximation; actual causation requires manual review

targets.rawSql:
WITH deploy_failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT df.deployment_id)::numeric /
  NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Change Fail Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deploy_failures df ON df.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND d.environment = '$environment'
AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Deployment Rework Rate (%)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of deployments that were followed by a hotfix deployment within 48 hours, indicating rework.

### What insight it offers
Frequent rework indicates that changes are not sufficiently tested before deployment. Reducing rework improves overall team efficiency.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Hotfix detection is based on deployment frequency within a short window, not labels
- May over-count in high-frequency deployment environments

targets.rawSql:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.created_at,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS next_deploy_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(CASE WHEN next_deploy_at - created_at < INTERVAL '48 hours' THEN 1 END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1
) AS "Rework Rate (%)"
FROM ranked

panels.title: Total Incidents
panels.description:
### What it measures
Total number of GitHub Issues labeled 'incident' created in the selected time range.

### What insight it offers
Absolute incident count provides context for the CFR metric and helps track service reliability over time.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Counts all incidents regardless of deployment linkage
- Requires consistent use of the 'incident' label in your GitHub workflow

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Incidents"
FROM issues i
WHERE i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
AND i.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Change Fail Rate over Time
panels.description:
### What it measures
Weekly change failure rate percentage trend for the selected environment.

### What insight it offers
Trending down indicates improving quality. Sudden spikes may correlate with large releases, team changes, or infrastructure events.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Weeks with very few deployments produce less reliable percentages
- Incident labeling must be consistent for trend accuracy

targets.rawSql:
WITH weekly_deploys AS (
  SELECT
    date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS week,
    d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
weekly_failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS week, d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  wd.week AS time,
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT wf.deployment_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT wd.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "CFR (%)"
FROM weekly_deploys wd
LEFT JOIN weekly_failures wf ON wf.week = wd.week AND wf.deployment_id = wd.deployment_id
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Incidents over Time
panels.description:
### What it measures
Weekly count of new incident-labeled issues created over time.

### What insight it offers
Aligning incident spikes with deployment activity reveals which releases caused the most disruption.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Incident creation date may lag the actual incident by hours
- Does not filter by environment (incidents are cross-environment)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', i.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(*) AS "Incidents"
FROM issues i
WHERE i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
AND i.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Open Incidents
panels.description:
### What it measures
List of currently open GitHub Issues labeled 'incident', ordered by most recently created.

### What insight it offers
Provides an at-a-glance view of active incidents that may still be impacting service reliability.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Only shows open issues; resolved incidents are excluded
- Requires the 'incident' label to be consistently applied

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  i.number AS "Issue #",
  i.title AS "Title",
  i.user_login AS "Reporter",
  i.created_at AS "Created At",
  i.state AS "State"
FROM issues i
WHERE i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
  AND i.state = 'open'
ORDER BY i.created_at DESC
LIMIT 50

panels.title: CFR: Copilot vs Non-Copilot
panels.description:
### What it measures
Change failure rate comparison between deployments linked to PRs by active Copilot users versus non-Copilot users.

### What insight it offers
If Copilot users have lower CFR, it may indicate that AI-assisted development leads to fewer defects reaching production.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Requires deployment-PR linkage
- Incident attribution uses the same 24-hour window heuristic

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
deploy_cohorts AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  dc.cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT f.deployment_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT dc.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "CFR (%)"
FROM deploy_cohorts dc
LEFT JOIN failures f ON f.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id
GROUP BY 1
